Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Cres Dp, Inc.'S Person (S) Most Knowledgeable To Appear For Deposition And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Nathaniel L. Dunn, Esq. of Chapman & Intrieri LLP, Tel. 510.864.3600, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF 139 STILLMAN STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel CRES DP, Inc.'s Person(s) Most Knowledgeable To Appear For Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion to compel non-party, CRES DP, Inc.'s "person most knowledgeable" to appear for oral deposition and produce documents is granted. The motion was unopposed. The moving party has confirmed personal service of a subpoena and the witness failed to appear for questioning on the designated day and location. The deposition shall take place within thirty days of the date of this order, or on such other later date as moving party and the witness-to-be-deposed shall agree. The corporation shall pay to the moving party the sum of $500.00 for failing to appear in compliance subpoena served on its agent for service. See CCP section 1992. The request for other monetary sanctions is denied. Failure of the witness to appear for deposition in compliance with this Order may result in punishment for contempt. CCP. section 2025.440(b), 2020.240. Moving party to submit a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
